subject: Public Health
title: Information Bias and Misclassification
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Information Bias and Misclassification.

hook.text: Ask people how much they drink and they'll underestimate. Ask about exercise and they'll overestimate.

buildup.text: Information bias occurs when data about exposure or disease is inaccurately measured or recorded.

discovery.text: Recall bias—people with disease remember exposures differently than healthy controls—is a major threat in case-control.

twist.text: Blinding—keeping participants and researchers unaware of group assignment—reduces information bias in trials.

climax.text: Standardized measurement tools, validated questionnaires, and objective biomarkers all help reduce information bias.

punchline.text: People are unreliable narrators of their own health—good studies account.

quiz.question: What is recall bias?
options: Sick people remember exposures differently than healthy people
